title: CSRF disabled without inline justification comment
description: .csrf { it.disable() } is correct for a stateless Bearer-token API but the rationale is not documented adjacent to the call. A future reviewer cannot confirm no endpoint relies on a session cookie for auth without tracing the whole filter chain.
recommendation: Add an inline comment on line 104 stating that CSRF is safely disabled because SessionCreationPolicy.STATELESS is set and all auth is via Bearer JWT with no session cookies.

title: No explicit CORS configuration -- Spring Boot implicit default
description: No CorsConfigurationSource bean is defined and http.cors() is not called. Spring Boot applies its default deny-all CORS policy which is safe today but is untestable and undocumented. Any future @CrossOrigin annotation on a controller could silently open unintended origins without a centralised review point.
recommendation: Define an explicit CorsConfigurationSource bean with allowedOrigins bound to \${PLUGWERK_BASE_URL}. Wire it into SecurityFilterChain with http.cors { it.configurationSource(corsConfigurationSource()) }.

title: AES-128 encryption key only -- no documented upgrade path to AES-256
description: Spring Encryptors.text() with a 16-character key uses AES-128-CBC. The @Size(min=16 max=16) constraint in AuthProperties hard-codes 16 chars and would reject a 32-char key preventing an in-place upgrade to AES-256. The design choice is not documented.
recommendation: Relax the @Size constraint to accept 16 or 32 chars. Document the AES-128 choice in an ADR or inline comment. Mark upgrade to AES-256 as a Phase-3 hardening item.

title: /actuator/info and /actuator/prometheus require only authentication -- no privileged role enforced
description: management.endpoints.web.exposure.include = health,info,prometheus. /actuator/health is explicitly permitAll. /actuator/info and /actuator/prometheus are not explicitly role-restricted and fall through to anyRequest().authenticated(). Any authenticated namespace member can retrieve Prometheus metrics and server build info.
recommendation: Add explicit requestMatchers for /actuator/info and /actuator/prometheus in SecurityConfiguration requiring a superadmin role or a dedicated METRICS authority.

title: Rate limiting covers only POST /api/v1/auth/login -- change-password endpoint is unprotected
description: shouldNotFilter() returns true for all URIs except exactly /api/v1/auth/login. POST /api/v1/auth/change-password accepts the current plaintext password as a credential and is not rate-limited. An attacker who has obtained a valid token can send unlimited change-password requests to brute-force the current password.
recommendation: Extend the rate-limit path list to include /api/v1/auth/change-password. Make the protected path set configurable in PlugwerkProperties.RateLimitProperties.

title: X-Forwarded-For accepted without proxy trust validation -- trivial IP-spoofing bypasses rate limit
description: resolveClientIp() reads the leftmost X-Forwarded-For value unconditionally regardless of whether the request came through a trusted proxy. A client can forge X-Forwarded-For: 1.2.3.4 and rotate forged IPs to bypass per-IP rate limit buckets entirely.
recommendation: Accept X-Forwarded-For only when remoteAddr is a member of a configurable trusted-proxy CIDR list (e.g. PLUGWERK_TRUSTED_PROXY_CIDRS). Fall back to remoteAddr when no trusted proxy is configured.

title: In-memory rate-limit state is node-local -- multi-replica deployments multiply the effective limit
description: LoginRateLimitService uses a JVM-local Caffeine cache. In a horizontally-scaled deployment with N replicas an attacker gets maxAttempts * N login attempts before any single replica blocks them.
recommendation: Document that the current implementation is single-node only. Provide an optional Redis-backed Bucket4j ProxyManager configuration (activated by a PLUGWERK_RATE_LIMIT_BACKEND=redis property) for multi-replica deployments.

title: Access key prefix lookup leaks timing side-channel -- valid prefixes are detectable by response latency
description: findByKeyPrefixAndRevokedFalse(prefix) issues a SQL SELECT before the constant-time passwordEncoder.matches() call. The DB round-trip timing differs between a hit (rows returned) and a miss (empty result). An attacker can enumerate valid 8-char prefixes by measuring HTTP response latency from outside the network.
recommendation: Replace the prefix-column approach with a full keyed-hash approach: store HMAC-SHA256(serverSideSecret, fullKey) in a dedicated hash column indexed for equality. Perform a single lookup by hash with no timing difference between found and not-found.

title: OIDC decoder fallback failures are silently swallowed -- no per-decoder audit trail
description: runCatching { decoder.decode(token) }.onSuccess { return it } suppresses each OIDC decoder exception without logging. A JWKS key rotation by an external provider will cause silent decode failures with no observable signal until users report authentication errors.
recommendation: Log each OIDC decoder failure at warn level including the decoder index or provider type and the exception message (never the raw token) before falling through to the next decoder.

title: GitHub OIDC decoder built without audience validation -- any GitHub Actions token is accepted
description: NimbusJwtDecoder.withJwkSetUri(GitHub JWKS URI).build() validates the signature but does not restrict the aud claim. GitHub Actions OIDC tokens carry an aud identifying the intended recipient. Without audience validation a token minted for a completely different workflow or repository is accepted as a valid Plugwerk credential.
recommendation: After building the NimbusJwtDecoder for GitHub configure a DelegatingOAuth2TokenValidator including JwtTimestampValidator and a JwtClaimValidator for the aud claim matching the expected Plugwerk server base URL or a dedicated audience value.

title: Facebook OIDC decoder built from JWKS URI only -- issuer claim not validated
description: NimbusJwtDecoder.withJwkSetUri(Facebook JWKS URI).build() checks the signature but not the iss claim. Any Facebook-signed JWT with a valid signature is accepted regardless of the Facebook service that issued it.
recommendation: Use JwtDecoders.fromIssuerLocation() for Facebook if OIDC discovery is supported. Otherwise add a JwtClaimValidator<String> checking iss == https://www.facebook.com inside a DelegatingOAuth2TokenValidator.

title: JWT signing key derived directly from UTF-8 bytes without key-derivation step
description: SecretKeySpec(bytes, HmacSHA256) uses raw UTF-8 bytes of jwtSecret with only a length check. If the secret is a low-entropy passphrase the effective key strength is less than 256 bits. No KDF or entropy enforcement is applied.
recommendation: Document in README and application.yml that PLUGWERK_JWT_SECRET must be produced with high entropy (e.g. openssl rand -base64 32). Optionally derive the final HMAC key via HKDF to guarantee full 256-bit key length independent of input encoding.

title: Revoked JTI stored as plain VARCHAR -- no hashing of the security identifier
description: The jti column stores the JWT ID as a plain UUID string (VARCHAR 36). A database dump or lower-privilege read exposes all revoked token IDs. While UUIDs are not directly exploitable the principle of defence-in-depth recommends hashing security identifiers at rest.
recommendation: Store SHA-256(jti) hex-encoded (64 chars) in the jti column. Update existsByJti and the Caffeine cache key to use the hashed value. Update the unique index accordingly.

title: requestURI prefix check may be bypassed via URL-encoded path segments
description: request.requestURI.startsWith(\/api/v1/auth/\") may not match URL-encoded variants such as /api/v1/auth%2F... on servlet containers that do not normalise the URI before filter execution. This could allow a request to bypass the password-change gate for auth endpoints."
recommendation: Use request.servletPath instead of requestURI -- the servlet spec requires the container to URL-decode and normalise servletPath. Add a test that sends an encoded-slash path to confirm the check holds.

title: BCryptPasswordEncoder uses default cost factor 10 -- OWASP recommends 12
description: BCryptPasswordEncoder() is constructed without an explicit strength parameter and defaults to cost factor 10. OWASP Password Storage Cheat Sheet (2024) recommends cost factor 12 for bcrypt on current server hardware to maintain adequate brute-force resistance.
recommendation: Change to BCryptPasswordEncoder(12). Benchmark sub-1s hashing on target production hardware. Consider a re-hash-on-login migration to upgrade existing hashes.

title: Tokens missing jti or sub bypass revocation silently with no audit log entry
description: checkRevocation() returns early without any action when jwt.id or jwt.subject is null. A JWT without these standard claims passes through revocation checks with no log entry. This is anomalous for any well-configured issuer and should be observable.
recommendation: Log a warn-level event when a token missing jti or sub is encountered so that misconfigured issuers are detectable.

title: AccessKeyController mutating endpoints (POST createAccessKey / DELETE revokeAccessKey) lack @PreAuthorize
description: No @PreAuthorize annotation is present on any method or the class. @EnableMethodSecurity is not active on SecurityConfiguration. Authorization is enforced only by inline namespaceAuthorizationService.requireRole() calls. A future refactor removing that call would silently drop the auth guard with no compile-time or AOP safety net.
recommendation: Enable @EnableMethodSecurity on SecurityConfiguration. Add @PreAuthorize(\isAuthenticated()\") at the class level as a floor guard. Add role-specific @PreAuthorize expressions on each mutating method. Retain programmatic namespaceAuthorizationService calls as the fine-grained layer."

title: AdminUserController mutating endpoints (POST createUser / PUT updateUser / DELETE deleteUser) lack @PreAuthorize
description: createUser updateUser and deleteUser rely entirely on inline requireSuperadmin() calls with no @PreAuthorize annotation. Same structural issue as SBS-AUTHZ-001.
recommendation: Same as SBS-AUTHZ-001.

title: NamespaceController mutating endpoints (POST createNamespace / PUT updateNamespace / DELETE deleteNamespace) lack @PreAuthorize
description: createNamespace updateNamespace and deleteNamespace have no @PreAuthorize on method or class. Same structural issue as SBS-AUTHZ-001.
recommendation: Same as SBS-AUTHZ-001.

title: NamespaceMemberController mutating endpoints (POST addMember / PUT updateMember / DELETE removeMember) lack @PreAuthorize
description: addNamespaceMember updateNamespaceMember and removeNamespaceMember have no @PreAuthorize. Same structural issue as SBS-AUTHZ-001.
recommendation: Same as SBS-AUTHZ-001.

title: OidcProviderController mutating endpoints (POST createOidcProvider / PUT updateOidcProvider / DELETE deleteOidcProvider) lack @PreAuthorize
description: createOidcProvider updateOidcProvider and deleteOidcProvider have no @PreAuthorize. These endpoints manage OIDC trust anchors -- a missing guard here would allow any authenticated user to register a malicious OIDC issuer that can then forge credentials for any user.
recommendation: Same as SBS-AUTHZ-001. Given the security criticality consider @PreAuthorize(\@namespaceAuthorizationService.isSuperadmin(authentication)\") explicitly on each method."

title: ManagementController mutating endpoints (POST uploadRelease / PATCH updatePlugin / PATCH updateReleaseStatus / DELETE deletePlugin / DELETE deleteRelease) lack @PreAuthorize
description: All five mutating methods have no @PreAuthorize annotation. Same structural issue as SBS-AUTHZ-001.
recommendation: Same as SBS-AUTHZ-001.

title: ReviewsController mutating endpoints (POST approveRelease / POST rejectRelease) lack @PreAuthorize
description: approveRelease and rejectRelease change plugin release status to PUBLISHED or YANKED respectively. Neither method has @PreAuthorize. Same structural issue as SBS-AUTHZ-001.
recommendation: Same as SBS-AUTHZ-001.

title: AdminSettingsController PATCH endpoint (updateApplicationSettings) lacks @PreAuthorize
description: updateApplicationSettings changes process-wide settings (upload limits, timezone, etc.) with no @PreAuthorize annotation. Authorization relies solely on the inline requireSuperadmin() helper. Same structural issue as SBS-AUTHZ-001.
recommendation: Same as SBS-AUTHZ-001.

title: UserSettingsController PATCH endpoint (updateUserSettings) lacks @PreAuthorize
description: updateUserSettings has no @PreAuthorize annotation. Access control relies solely on the inline requireAuthenticatedSubject() helper. Same structural issue as SBS-AUTHZ-001.
recommendation: Same as SBS-AUTHZ-001.
